description: Historical motor insurance claims with outcomes. Used for RAG-based precedent lookup.

claims.description: Customer vehicle stationary at traffic signal when struck from behind by another vehicle. Airbags did not deploy. Driver uninjured.
claims.lesson: Classic rear-end collision. Damage pattern (rear only, tail lights + bumper + boot) perfectly consistent with stationary impact. Clean policy history. Approve with standard deductible.

claims.description: Vehicle reversed into parked truck in office parking. Damage to rear. Driver noticed while reversing camera feed was obscured.
fraud_indicators: One prior claim on policy
claims.lesson: Reversing damage limited to rear-left area is physically consistent. One prior claim not disqualifying. Approved with depreciation deduction on door panel.

claims.description: Head-on collision with oncoming truck that crossed the median. Front severely damaged. Driver hospitalized with minor injuries. FIR filed.
fraud_indicators: Large claim amount
claims.lesson: Severe front-end collision with airbag deployment consistent with head-on high-speed impact. FIR corroborated the incident. Large claim justified by damage extent.

claims.description: Claimant reported vehicle submerged during flash flooding. Engine and electrical system damage claimed.
fraud_indicators: Policy issued only 23 days before claim
IMD weather data: no significant rainfall at BTM Layout on claimed date
Vehicle service records show pre-existing engine coolant leak
No water damage visible on exterior panels
claims.lesson: New policy (23 days) + weather data contradiction + pre-existing engine issue = clear fraud. Cross-checking IMD weather records is critical for flood and weather claims.

claims.description: Vehicle lane-driving on flyover sideswiped by KSRTC bus merging from left. Entire right side affected.
claims.lesson: Consistent linear sideswiping damage along right panels. KSRTC bus depot confirmed incident via route log. Third party claim.

claims.description: Collision at uncontrolled intersection. Claimant ran red light. Damage to front-left quadrant.
fraud_indicators: Claim filed 4 days after incident without medical reason
Workshop estimate 42% above MAIA market rate
No FIR despite significant intersection damage
claims.lesson: Genuine damage but inflated estimate. Late reporting and no FIR despite major accident are yellow flags. Settled at market rate with deductible. Workshop was non-empanelled.

claims.description: Vehicle reported stolen from residential parking overnight. Total loss claim filed.
fraud_indicators: Second theft claim on this policy in 5 years
FASTag records show vehicle crossing tolls in Rajasthan 3 days after reported theft
FIR filed in wrong jurisdiction (different police station from reported location)
Claimant failed to produce duplicate key as required
claims.lesson: FASTag data directly contradicted theft claim. Serial theft claimant. Always cross-check FASTag records for theft claims on high-value vehicles.

claims.description: Truck failed to stop and struck the rear of customer vehicle on elevated flyover causing secondary impact with divider.
claims.lesson: Flyover CCTV confirmed rear-end collision. Rear + left-side damage consistent with secondary divider impact after primary rear strike.

claims.description: Swerved to avoid pothole, hit central divider. Front-left damage.
fraud_indicators: Minor directionality inconsistency in damage — door damage slightly more severe than divider impact would typically cause
claims.lesson: Left-side damage consistent with hitting left divider while swerving right to avoid pothole. Minor inconsistency insufficient for escalation. Pothole reported by municipality confirms road condition.

claims.description: Front-end collision with stationary vehicle in slow-moving traffic.
fraud_indicators: Policy only 12 days old
Rust and oxidation visible on damaged headlights and bumper brackets — damage predates policy
Damage extent inconsistent with described low-speed city traffic collision
No FIR filed, third party vehicle not identified
claims.lesson: Textbook new-policy fraud. Rust on damaged parts proves pre-existing damage. 12-day-old policy with no pre-inception survey. Rejected.

claims.description: Vehicle parked in open lot during severe hailstorm. Uniform dent damage across horizontal surfaces.
claims.lesson: IMD confirmed severe hailstorm in Hinjewadi area on claimed date. Uniform dent pattern on horizontal surfaces (bonnet, roof, boot) is hallmark of hail damage. Straightforward approval.

claims.description: Intersection collision with another vehicle. Both parties claim each other at fault.
fraud_indicators: Third party vehicle also insured by the same insurer (same branch)
Both drivers from same residential locality — Yelahanka Satellite Town
Visible damage minimal relative to claim amount of ₹95,000
No police FIR despite reported intersection accident
Both vehicles brought to same non-empanelled workshop
claims.lesson: Social network analysis revealed claimants are neighbours. Same insurer, same locality, same workshop, inflated estimate — staged collision. Both claims rejected.

claims.description: Vehicle rolled over while overtaking on highway. Multiple panels damaged. Driver sustained injuries, hospitalised.
fraud_indicators: Large claim amount requiring mandatory surveyor
claims.lesson: Rollover results in characteristic all-around damage pattern across all panels and pillars. Damage fully consistent. Hospital records corroborated. Large legitimate claim.

claims.description: Vehicle parked in gated society lot. Found damaged in morning — hit by unknown vehicle overnight.
fraud_indicators: Second hit-and-run claim in 18 months in the same gated community
No CCTV footage produced from the security-controlled parking
Controlled-access parking makes unknown vehicle scenario unlikely
claims.lesson: Damage is genuine but pattern of hit-and-run claims in controlled environment raises concern. Reduced settlement after enhanced review.

claims.description: Vehicle submerged up to dashboard during Chennai monsoon floods. Engine seized, interior damage.
claims.lesson: Chennai monsoon event affected thousands of vehicles. Area-level flood confirmed by IMD and NDRF records. Waterline on interior consistent with reported submersion level. Approved.

claims.description: Vehicle hit deep pothole at night causing underbody, suspension, and alloy wheel damage.
claims.lesson: Pothole damage pattern on left-front underbody and suspension consistent with deep pothole impact. BBMP pothole report for that road section available as evidence.

claims.description: Key-scratch damage covering entire right side of vehicle. Found at commercial parking.
fraud_indicators: Policy 45 days old
Scratch pattern suspiciously uniform and deep — tool-assisted rather than random keying
No witnesses or CCTV in paid parking lot (unusual for commercial area)
claims.lesson: Scratch damage difficult to distinguish from deliberate self-damage. Relatively new policy adds suspicion. Partial settlement pending police report.

claims.description: Collided with deer crossing highway at night. Front and bonnet damage.
claims.lesson: Forest department confirmed active deer crossing zone on that road section. Damage height and pattern consistent with deer-strike at speed. Bonnet and windshield crack pattern typical of animal impact.

claims.description: Rear-end damage claimed after parking lot incident.
fraud_indicators: Three prior claims on this policy
Same workshop used for all four claims — non-empanelled
Damage severity on photos inconsistent with workshop estimate of ₹85,000
Workshop estimate for identical damage was ₹28,000 at empanelled garage
claims.lesson: Genuine collision but serial workshop inflation pattern. Claim escalated to SIU for workshop investigation. Policy flagged for non-renewal.

claims.description: Side-swipe collision on highway when neighbouring car changed lanes without signal. Left side and front-left damage.
claims.lesson: Left-side linear damage from front fender through both doors consistent with side-swipe at highway speed. Third party fled scene but dashcam footage submitted as evidence.
